body.title: [sprint] audit-ticket SPLITs any ticket with >1 falsifiable intention or no writable gate
body.intention: Given a ticket, /audit-ticket returns SPLIT when it carries more than one falsifiable intention OR when no single gate a hollow build couldn't pass can be written for it.
body.description:
**Problem:** 'One falsifiable intention per ticket' is only a hope unless something enforces it at filing time. The gate-writability test ('can I write one gate a hollow build can't pass for this whole ticket?') is the self-terminating atomicity criterion — it belongs in /audit-ticket as a SPLIT trigger.

**Proposed shape:** Add an atomicity check to /audit-ticket: (1) does the ticket state exactly one falsifiable intention (the `intention` field)? (2) is it gate-writable? If two intentions or non-gate-writable → SPLIT with suggested child boundaries. Atomic≠tiny: 'round-trips' is one intention though it spans write+read — key on one falsifiable *claim*, not smallest diff.

**Affected files:** `skills/audit-ticket/SKILL.md` (+ ~/.claude/skills mirror) — MEDIUM inertia; any audit-ticket helper.
**Design rules:** skills-live-in-repo (repo skills/ canonical); prove-what's-load-bearing. Be honest re mechanical vs model-scored: presence of one `intention` is mechanical; gate-writability is a model judgement.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the two-part atomicity check + SPLIT verdict. OUT — the proof emitter/close-gate; the CP-tag check (separate ticket); rewriting the rest of audit-ticket.
**Test plan:** Feed a 2-intention ticket → SPLIT. Feed a 1-intention gate-writable ticket → no SPLIT on the atomicity axis. Feed a vague 'improve X / use judgement' ticket → SPLIT.
**Completion criteria:** /audit-ticket on the three fixtures returns SPLIT, PASS-on-axis, SPLIT; the gate-writability question appears verbatim in the skill.
